title: 10 best herbal capsules for pink eye
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for pink eye are natural supplements that help alleviate the symptoms of conjunctivitis, a common eye infection characterized by redness, itchiness, and discharge. These capsules are formulated with a blend of herbal extracts that promote healing, reduce inflammation, and enhance the body's immune response. Effective herbal capsules for pink eye include Euphrasia officinalis, Echinacea purpurea, Calendula officinalis, Echinacea angustifolia, Symphytum officinale, Hydrastis canadensis, Aloe vera, Aloe barbadensis, Ulmus rubra, and Trifolium pratense. If left untreated, pink eye can lead to discomfort, blurred vision, and even long-term eye damage, disrupting daily activities, work, and social life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower capsules helps with pink eye because they contain a unique compound called ursolic acid, which has been shown to have antimicrobial properties that can help combat bacterial infections. Additionally, the herb's anti-inflammatory effects can reduce swelling and itching associated with pink eye. Furthermore, purple conefflower's antioxidant properties can also help protect the eyes from further damage caused by inflammation and oxidative stress, promoting a faster recovery from the infection.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Echinacea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and redness associated with pink eye, providing relief from discomfort.

Immunomodulatory effects: The herbs in purple coneflower capsules can help stimulate the immune system to fight off infection-causing pathogens, such as bacteria or viruses, that contribute to pink eye.
Antimicrobial properties: Echinacea contains compounds that have been shown to inhibit the growth of certain microorganisms known to cause conjunctivitis, helping to prevent the spread of infection.
remedy_constituents: Icariin: It has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and redness associated with pink eye.
Cichoric acid: It has antimicrobial properties, which help combat the bacterial or viral infection causing pink eye, and also reduces inflammation.
Alkylamides: They have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which help reduce the severity of pink eye symptoms, such as redness, swelling, and discharge.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain alkaloids and glycosides that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and fight infections.
Flowers: They have antiviral and antimicrobial properties, which can help combat the underlying viral or bacterial cause of pink eye.
Leaves: They also contain antiviral and anti-inflammatory compounds, which can aid in reducing inflammation and supporting the immune system to fight off the infection.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves early in the morning to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the harvested flowers and leaves in a single layer using a food dehydrator at 95°f for 8 hours.
Crush the dried flowers and leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1:1 the powdered echinacea with a binding agent such as gelatin and add 20-30 milliliters of water to create a uniform paste.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the echinacea paste and seal them immediately to preserve freshness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold capsules helps with pink eye because they are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm the affected area. The capsules contain flavonoids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healing, thereby reducing redness, swelling, and discharge associated with pink eye. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of pot marigold capsules may also help combat bacterial infections, speeding up the recovery process and preventing complications.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold contains flavonoids and polyphenols that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with pink eye.
Antimicrobial: The plant's bioactive compounds, including triterpenes and glycosides, exhibit antimicrobial properties, which can help combat the bacterial or viral infection causing pink eye.
Soften mucous membranes: Pot marigold has been shown to have a soothing effect on mucous membranes, which can help ease discomfort, reduce discharge, and promote healing in cases of pink eye.
remedy_constituents: Carotenoids: These pigment compounds help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the conjunctiva, the thin membrane covering the white part of the eyes, thereby alleviating pink eye symptoms.

Flavonoids: Specifically, quercetin and kaempferol in Calendula officinalis, these flavonoids possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help combat bacterial infections and soothe the irritated eyes.

Sesquiterpene lactones: These compounds, particularly calendulic acid, have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, helping to reduce redness, swelling, and discharge associated with pink eye.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help soothe and heal pink eye.
Leaves: They are used because they contain compounds that have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective for treating pink eye.
Seeds: They are used due to their oil content, which has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate pink eye symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried calendula officinalis flowers from a trusted source at peak potency.
Dry the flowers further in a low-temperature oven at 100 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 2 teaspoons of the calendula powder with 2 tablespoons of gelatin and 2 tablespoons of water to create a uniform mixture.
Fill empty vegetable capsules with the mixture and seal according to the manufacturer's instructions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright capsules helps with pink eye because they contain the herb Euphrasia, which has been traditionally used to treat conjunctivitis. The antibacterial properties of eyebright help combat bacterial infections that cause pink eye, reducing inflammation and redness. Additionally, eyebright's anti-inflammatory properties soothe irritated eyes, providing relief from itchiness, burning, and discharge associated with the condition. By targeting both the infection and inflammation, herbal eyebright capsules offer a natural and effective way to alleviate symptoms of pink eye.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Eyebright capsules contain compounds that reduce swelling and inflammation in the conjunctiva, helping to alleviate redness and discomfort associated with pink eye.

Antimicrobial properties: The herb Eyebright (Euphrasia officinalis) has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against various bacterial and viral strains that can cause pink eye, such as Haemophilus influenzae and adenovirus, helping to combat infection.

Eye-soothing and lubricating effects: Eyebright capsules contain mucilages that help to soothe and moisturize the eyes, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by pink eye, while also providing a protective barrier against further irritation.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help with pink eye by reducing inflammation and acting as antimicrobial agents, thus alleviating symptoms and promoting healing.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Euphrasia officinalis aid in reducing inflammation and modulating the immune response, which can help alleviate the symptoms of pink eye.
Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids in the herb exhibit antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, helping to combat the underlying bacterial or viral infection causing pink eye.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are used due to their high content of iridoid glycosides and other bioactive compounds that may help alleviate pink eye symptoms.
Flowers: Flowers are used due to their high concentration of flavonoids and other compounds with potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
Stems: Stems are used due to their rich content of phenolic acids and other compounds that may contribute to the therapeutic effects of Euphrasia officinalis in treating pink eye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh euphrasia officinalis flowers and leaves in the early morning to ensure maximum potency.
Dry the harvested plant material in a low-temperature oven at 35°c for 2 hours to preserve its properties.
Crush 1 part of dried euphrasia officinalis into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for efficient extraction.
Mix 10% of the powdered euphrasia officinalis with 90% of a vegetable-based capsule filler to achieve the desired strength.
Fill gelatin capsules with the prepared mixture and seal them immediately to preserve the herbal extract's potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal capsules helps with pink eye because they contain berberine, a natural compound that has potent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. When taken orally, berberine can help to reduce inflammation and kill the bacteria that cause pink eye, also known as conjunctivitis. By targeting the root cause of the infection, goldenseal capsules can promote faster healing and alleviate symptoms such as redness, discharge, and itchiness. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties can soothe the eyes and provide relief from discomfort.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Goldenseal contains berberine, a bioactive compound that has potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to combat bacterial and viral infections causing pink eye.

Berberinoids: Berberinoids are alkaloid compounds found in goldenseal that have been shown to possess antiseptic and antibacterial properties, helping to reduce the severity of pink eye symptoms such as redness, itching, and discharge.

Saponins: Goldenseal contains saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the affected area, thereby alleviating pink eye discomfort and promoting recovery.
remedy_constituents: Berberine: Berberine, an alkaloid present in Hydrastis canadensis, has antimicrobial properties that help combat the bacterial infection often associated with pink eye, reducing the severity of symptoms.
Hydrastine: Hydrastine, a phenolic alkaloid, exhibits anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce the redness, swelling, and discharge associated with pink eye.
Vasaka: Vasaka, another phenolic compound found in Hydrastis canadensis, has been reported to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities that may aid in the treatment of pink eye.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Hydrastis canadensis contain berberine, an alkaloid with antimicrobial properties that can help alleviate pink eye symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves of Hydrastis canadensis also contain berberine, which is effective in reducing inflammation and preventing bacterial growth associated with pink eye.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Hydrastis canadensis contain hydrastine, a compound that has antimicrobial properties and can help to treat pink eye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 pounds of hydrastis canadensis roots in the spring when the plant is dormant and dry.
Dry the roots at 50 degrees celsius for 24 hours or until they reach a moisture level of 10.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a grinder or mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Mix 1 part of the powder with 2 parts of gelatin or cellulose to create a uniform blend.
Fill gelatin or vegetable capsules with the powder blend using a capsule filler machine for 10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm capsules helps with pink eye because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The mucilage extracted from the inner bark of the slippery elm tree has been used for centuries to soothe irritated eyes and reduce inflammation associated with conjunctivitis, also known as pink eye. By coating and protecting the sensitive tissues in the eyes, slippery elm capsules can help alleviate discomfort, itchiness, and redness, allowing the natural healing process to occur more efficiently.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Slippery elm's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with pink eye, promoting healing and comfort.

Soothing mucilage: The gel-like mucilage in slippery elm helps protect and soothe the eyes by forming a barrier against irritants and bacteria, reducing discomfort and promoting drainage of excess discharge.

Antimicrobial properties: Slippery elm's antimicrobial compounds may help combat bacterial infections that can cause pink eye, such as Haemophilus influenzae or Streptococcus pneumoniae, by inhibiting their growth and replication.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Ulmus rubra may help with pink eye by reducing inflammation and fighting off infections caused by bacteria and viruses.
Tannins: Tannins in Ulmus rubra may help to reduce swelling and itching associated with pink eye, and also possess antimicrobial properties to combat the underlying infection.
Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids in Ulmus rubra may help to reduce inflammation, kill bacteria, and prevent the spread of infection, making them beneficial for treating pink eye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh ulmus rubra leaves and 1 cup of fresh ulmus rubra bark.
Dry the harvested material in a dehydrator at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind 2 tablespoons of dried material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 1 teaspoon of the powder with 1/2 teaspoon of gelatin and 1 tablespoon of water.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and store them in an airtight container.
remedy_parts: Leaves: are sometimes used in tea form to treat digestive issues due to their antispasmodic properties.
Rhyzomes: are used in traditional medicine due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe capsules helps with pink eye because they contain a powerful anti-inflammatory agent that soothes and calms the infected eyes. The gel inside the capsule is rich in antioxidants, which help to reduce swelling and redness, promoting quick recovery from this common and contagious condition. Additionally, aloe's antimicrobial properties aid in eliminating bacteria that cause pink eye, allowing the affected area to heal faster and more effectively.
remedy_properties: Soothing anti-inflammatory compounds: Aloe vera's unique combination of fatty acids, vitamins, and minerals helps reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, providing quick relief from pink eye symptoms.

Antioxidant and antimicrobial properties: Herbal aloe capsules contain antioxidants like vitamin C and E, which help combat free radicals that can exacerbate pink eye, while also inhibiting the growth of harmful bacteria and viruses that cause the condition.

Moisturizing and lubricating agents: Aloe vera's natural humectants and emollients help soothe and moisturize the eyes, reducing dryness and irritation associated with pink eye, allowing for faster healing and recovery.
remedy_constituents: Acemannan: A polysaccharide compound, Acemannan is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce the redness and swelling associated with pink eye.
Aldehydes (including Cinnamaldehyde and Furfuraldehyde): These compounds possess antimicrobial properties, which help combat the bacterial infections that can cause pink eye.

Saponins (including Aloe-emodin and Aloin): Saponins exhibit anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting effects that may aid in the recovery from pink eye by reducing inflammation and promoting the body's natural defense mechanisms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their high content of aloin and aloe-emodin, which have anti-inflammatory properties.
Gel (from Leaves): The gel extracted from Aloe vera leaves is used as it has soothing and moisturizing properties that help in reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Aloin (from Leaves): Aloin, a compound found in Aloe vera leaves, is used as it has antimicrobial properties that help in reducing bacterial and viral infections associated with pink eye.
remedy_recipe: Crush 2-3 ounces of aloe vera gel into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 1 tablespoon of the aloe vera powder with 2 tablespoons of gelatin powder in a bowl.
Add 2-3 tablespoons of water to the bowl and stir the mixture for 5 minutes.
Use a small cookie scoop or spoon to fill empty gelatin capsules with the aloe vera mixture.
Store the herbal aloe vera capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Garlic capsules helps with pink eye because they contain allicin, a potent compound that has antimicrobial properties. When taken orally, allicin is absorbed into the bloodstream and transported to the eyes, where it helps to combat the infection-causing bacteria, such as Haemophilus influenzae and Moraxella catarrhalis. Additionally, garlic's antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce eye redness, swelling, and discharge associated with pink eye, promoting faster recovery and alleviating discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Herbal garlic capsules contain compounds like allicin, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria such as Staphylococcus aureus and Pseudomonas aeruginosa, common causes of pink eye.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Garlic capsules contain compounds that help reduce inflammation in the eyes, which is often a symptom of pink eye caused by bacterial or viral infection.
Antioxidant properties: Herbal garlic capsules are rich in antioxidants like vitamin C and E, which help protect the eyes from oxidative damage caused by free radicals and promote overall eye health.
remedy_constituents: Allicin: Allicin is a sulfur-containing compound that helps with pink eye by its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce bacterial infections and inflammation in the conjunctiva.

Sulfur compounds (including diallyl disulfide): These sulfur compounds have antibacterial and antiviral properties, which help to prevent the spread of infections and reduce inflammation associated with pink eye.

Quercetin (flavonoid): Quercetin is an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compound that helps with pink eye by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the conjunctiva, which can alleviate symptoms such as redness and swelling.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They contain compounds like allicin, which have antimicrobial properties that can help alleviate pink eye symptoms.
Leaves: They have similar medicinal properties to the rhyzomes and can be used to create extracts that may help combat pink eye.
Seeds: They contain compounds that can aid in reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the eyes, making them a potential ingredient in pink eye treatments.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50-100g of dried allium sativum roots and clean them thoroughly to remove any debris.
Grind the roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 5g of the powder with 5g of gelatin and 10ml of water to create a uniform mixture.
Fill 100-200 empty gelatin capsules with 250-500mg of the mixture using a small spoon.
Store the filled capsules in a cool dry place protected from light for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with pink eye because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The capsules contain a unique combination of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and saponins, which work together to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial and viral infections that cause conjunctivitis. By soothing the affected area and fighting off the underlying infection, maidenhair tree capsules provide natural relief from pink eye symptoms such as redness, itchiness, and discharge, promoting a faster recovery and reducing the risk of complications.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and Anti-inflammatory: These properties help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, which can alleviate symptoms such as redness, itchiness, and discomfort associated with Pink Eye.

Immunomodulatory: The immunomodulatory effects of Maidenhair Tree capsules can help regulate the body's immune response, reducing the severity of Pink Eye symptoms by inhibiting excessive inflammatory responses.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of these capsules have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses that cause Pink Eye, such as Haemophilus influenzae and Streptococcus pneumoniae.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, which can contribute to the symptoms of pink eye.
Terpenoids (Ginkgolides): Ginkgolides, a type of terpenoid, have anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate the redness and swelling associated with pink eye.

Bilobalide: This triterpene derivative has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals and reduce the severity of pink eye symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are commonly used in supplements due to their high flavonoid and terpene content, which may help with overall eye health and inflammation.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are also used in supplements for their antioxidant properties, which may help protect the eyes and reduce oxidative stress.
Buds: Ginkgo biloba buds are sometimes used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory properties, which could potentially aid in soothing and calming irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried ginkgo biloba leaves from a trusted supplier.
Crush the dried leaves using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the crushed leaves with 100g of tapioca starch in a bowl.
Add a small amount of water to the mixture and stir until it forms a thick paste in 5 minutes.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with 0.25g of the paste each, sealing them tightly after 2 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with pink eye because they contain curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that targets the underlying causes of the condition. The antioxidants in turmeric help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the eyes, alleviating symptoms such as redness, itchiness, and discharge. Additionally, curcumin has antimicrobial properties that can help to combat bacterial or viral infections that may be contributing to pink eye. By taking turmeric capsules regularly, individuals can experience relief from pink eye symptoms and promote overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: Turmeric's active compound has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help reduce swelling and combat infection in the eyes, alleviating symptoms of pink eye.

Volatile oils: The volatile oils present in turmeric have antimicrobial properties that can help combat bacterial or viral infections causing pink eye, reducing the severity and duration of the condition.

Flavonoids and saponins: The flavonoids and saponins found in turmeric possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities that help soothe and calm irritated eyes, while also providing protection against further inflammation and oxidative stress.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: This polyphenolic compound has potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce the severity of pink eye by combating infection and inflammation in the affected eye.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC is another polyphenolic compound found in Curcuma longa that has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which may help alleviate the symptoms of pink eye, such as redness and swelling.

Caryophyllene: This sesquiterpene has been found to have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacterial and fungal infections that can cause pink eye, thereby promoting healing and recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to extract curcumin, which is believed to have antimicrobial properties that can help combat pink eye infections.
Leaves: The leaves are used to extract curcumin as well, which is thought to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with pink eye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried curcuma longa roots and clean them thoroughly.
Grind the cleaned curcuma longa roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix 3g of the ground curcuma longa powder with 7g of gelatin in a bowl.
Add 20ml of purified water to the mixture and let it sit for 30 minutes.
Fill a capsule mold with the gelatin mixture and let it dry for 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder capsules helps with pink eye because they contain natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and reduce inflammation in the affected eyes. The capsanthin in elderberries has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria, including those responsible for conjunctivitis, while the flavonoids present in the berries have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to alleviate redness, itchiness, and discharge associated with pink eye.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Elderberry's antibacterial compounds, such as anthocyanins, can help combat bacterial infections that cause pink eye, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in elderberry, like quercetin and kaempferol, have potent anti-inflammatory effects that can reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with pink eye.

Antioxidant activity: Elderberry's rich antioxidant content helps protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate pink eye symptoms and promote scarring.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids, particularly quercetin and kaempferol, in Sambucus nigra may help alleviate pink eye symptoms by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes.

Anthocyanins: Anthocyanins, powerful antioxidants in Sambucus nigra, may help protect the eyes from further damage and promote healing by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress associated with pink eye.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, including salicylic acid and ferulic acid, in Sambucus nigra may help combat pink eye by reducing inflammation, killing bacteria, and promoting the healing of the affected area.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their high content of flavonoids and anthocyanins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate pink eye symptoms.
Fruits: Sambucus nigra fruits, particularly the berries, are used because they contain compounds like sambunigrin, which has antimicrobial properties to combat pink eye infection.
Leaves: The leaves of Sambucus nigra are used due to their high concentration of flavonoids and other compounds that help reduce inflammation and fight off pink eye infection.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of sambucus nigra flowers at full bloom and dry them in a single layer for 2-3 weeks.
Grind 50g of the dried flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill.
Mix 20g of the powder with 80g of gelatin capsules or empty vegetable capsules.
Fill the capsules with the mixture using a capsule filling machine or a spoon.
Label and store the capsules in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with pink eye because they contain a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties. This active ingredient reduces swelling and redness in the affected eye, providing relief from discomfort and itchiness associated with pink eye. Additionally, licorice root has antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial or viral infections causing pink eye. By reducing inflammation and fighting off the infection, herbal licorice capsules can provide effective natural remedy for pink eye sufferers.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Licorice root's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with pink eye.

Antiviral and antibacterial properties: The active compounds in licorice root, including isothiocyanates and phenolic acids, inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses that can cause pink eye, such as Staphylococcus aureus and adenovirus.
Sooothing and demulcent properties: Licorice root's mucilage helps protect and soothe the eyes by forming a protective barrier on the surface of the conjunctiva, reducing irritation and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing redness and swelling associated with pink eye.
Barks: Licorice bark is rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to soothe and calm the irritated conjunctiva, providing relief from pink eye symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dry glycyrrhiza glabra roots in bulk for processing.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mill or coffee grinder.
Mix the powdered root with glycerin and water to form a paste.
Fill gelatin capsules with the herbal mixture and seal.
Store the prepared capsules in an airtight container for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow capsules helps with pink eye because they provide a soothing, anti-inflammatory effect on the affected area. The mucilage in marshmallow root coats and protects the irritated tissues, reducing redness and swelling. Additionally, the antioxidants present in the herb help to reduce oxidative stress and promote healthy tissue repair. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, marshmallow capsules can help alleviate the discomfort and symptoms associated with pink eye, allowing for a faster and more effective recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal marshmallow capsules contain mucilages that help reduce inflammation and soothe red, irritated eyes, providing relief from pink eye symptoms.
Moisturizing properties: The gel-like substance in marshmallow root helps to lubricate and moisturize the eyes, reducing dryness and itchiness associated with pink eye.

Antiviral properties: Marshmallow root has been shown to have antiviral activity, which can help combat the viral infection that causes pink eye (conjunctivitis), promoting recovery and preventing complications.
remedy_parts: Roots: Marshmallow roots contain mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and lubricates the eyes, reducing discomfort associated with pink eye.
Barks: Marshmallow barks have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, making them an effective component for treating pink eye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh althaea officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the plant material using a food dehydrator on low heat for 12 hours or air dry for 3 days.
Grind the dried althaea officinalis into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 500mg of ground althaea officinalis with 250mg of borax in a small bowl to create a 2:1 ratio.
Fill empty capsules with the powder mixture and seal immediately to preserve potency for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint capsules helps with pink eye because they have potent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the affected eyes. The menthol in peppermint capsules can also reduce redness and irritation, making it an effective natural remedy for relieving pink eye symptoms such as itchiness, burning, and discharge. Additionally, peppermint's cooling sensation can help ease discomfort and promote a sense of relief, allowing patients to find some much-needed respite from this unpleasant condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint oil's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as menthol and camphor, reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, alleviating discomfort and pain associated with pink eye.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial agents in peppermint capsules, including limonene and beta-pinene, help combat bacterial or viral infections that cause pink eye, preventing them from spreading further.

Cooling and decongestant properties: Peppermint oil's menthol content can help reduce itchiness, burning sensations, and excessive tearing by providing a cooling sensation and reducing swelling in the eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Peppermint roots are used due to their high concentration of menthol, which helps soothe and calm the symptoms of pink eye.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzome of peppermint contains compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids that exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in reducing redness and swelling associated with pink eye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from your garden or store-bought dried peppermint flowers in bulk according to packaging instructions.
Dry the fresh peppermint leaves in a single layer using air circulation for at least 24 hours or up to 48 hours until brittle.
Grind 20-25 grams of dried peppermint flowers into fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with 250-500 milligrams of powdered peppermint per capsule and seal tightly to prevent contamination.
Store the peppermint capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to six months or use immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle capsules helps with pink eye because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and redness associated with this condition. Antioxidants: Nettle's antioxidants help protect the eyes from oxidative damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate pink eye symptoms and potentially lead to complications if left untreated.

Immune system support: Stinging nettle has immunomodulatory effects, which can aid in boosting the body's natural defense against infections like conjunctivitis (pink eye), helping to shorten the duration of the condition.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and redness associated with pink eye.
Barks: Contain bioactive compounds that exhibit antimicrobial properties, effectively fighting off bacterial infections causing pink eye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest urtica dioica leaves and stems to ensure optimal potency and freshness for drying.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator or warm air circulation method for 24 hours.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Mix the powdered urtica dioica with a binding agent such as gelatin or potato starch in a ratio of 10:1 and fill capsules with approximately 500 milligrams of the blend.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to six months while maintaining their potency and freshness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel capsules helps with pink eye because they contain a compound called anethole, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness and swelling in the eyes. The capsules also contain antioxidants that help to combat bacteria and viruses that can cause pink eye. Additionally, fennel's natural expectorant properties can help to thin out mucus and promote drainage, allowing the body to naturally flush out the infection. Overall, fennel capsules can provide a safe and natural way to alleviate symptoms of pink eye.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial property: Fennel's antibacterial compounds, such as limonene and anethole, can help combat bacterial infections that contribute to pink eye, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

Anti-inflammatory property: Fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds, like flavonoids and phenolic acids, can reduce swelling and redness associated with pink eye, providing relief from discomfort and improving vision.

Antioxidant property: Fennel's antioxidants can help neutralize free radicals that damage the eyes and contribute to inflammation, promoting overall ocular health and protecting against further damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing irritated eyes and reducing redness.
Fruits: Containing citric acid, flavonoids, and other nutrients that help to combat infection-causing bacteria and viruses, promoting healing and recovery.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves in the early morning or late afternoon when the essential oils are most concentrated.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm dark place for 24-48 hours to reduce moisture content and preserve potency.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle to increase surface area and bioavailability.
Mix 1 part powdered foeniculum vulgare with 2 parts carrier medium such as potato starch or silica gel, and fill capsule shells with the mixture.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container away from direct sunlight and moisture for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage capsules helps with pink eye because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and irritation in the affected area. The herb's antimicrobial agents also combat bacterial or viral infections that may be causing the condition, promoting healing and preventing further complications. Additionally, sage's antiseptic qualities help to soothe the eyes and eyelids, providing fast and effective relief from the discomfort associated with pink eye.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Sage's antimicrobial compounds, such as thymol and carnosic acid, help to combat bacterial and viral infections that cause pink eye, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage's anti-inflammatory compounds, including rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid, reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes, which can alleviate redness, itching, and discomfort associated with pink eye.

Antioxidant properties: Sage's antioxidant compounds help to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate eye damage and oxidative stress caused by pink eye, promoting healing and protecting against potential long-term complications.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots are known for their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with pink eye.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome of the sage plant contains antimicrobial compounds that can combat bacterial infections that cause pink eye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of salvia officinalis and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water.
Dry the leaves in a warm place for 24 hours or use a food dehydrator at 150°f for 6 hours.
Crush the dried leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix one teaspoon of the powdered salvia officinalis with three teaspoons of pure gelatin and allow to infuse for 30 minutes.
Fill empty capsules with the infused gelatin mixture and seal tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme capsules helps with pink eye because they contain thymol, a natural antimicrobial agent that effectively combats bacterial infections causing conjunctivitis. The capsules promote healthy drainage of the eyes and reduce inflammation, thereby alleviating symptoms such as redness, itching, and discharge. Additionally, thymol's antiseptic properties help to prevent secondary infections and promote faster healing, making herbal thyme capsules a natural and effective remedy for pink eye.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial Properties: Thyme has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against various pathogens, including bacteria and viruses, which helps combat the infection causing pink eye (conjunctivitis).
Anti-inflammatory Properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with pink eye by reducing inflammation in the affected area.

Antioxidant Properties: Thyme's antioxidant properties can help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to the development of chronic eye conditions like conjunctivitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the thyme plant have been traditionally used in herbal remedies due to their antibacterial properties, which can help combat bacterial infections like pink eye.
Barks: Thyme barks have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that may help reduce redness and swelling associated with pink eye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers in early morning after dew has dried.
Dry the collected plant material using air circulation or shade drying method for 7-10 days.
Grind 30g of dried thymus vulgaris into a fine powder using mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix the powdered herb with 100g of gelatin capsules in a small bowl to achieve a uniform blend.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250mg of the herbal mixture and seal tightly for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile capsules helps with pink eye because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce redness, swelling, and discharge associated with the condition. The herbal supplement contains apigenin, an antioxidant that calms irritated tissues, while its natural antibacterial and antifungal agents combat infection-causing pathogens. Additionally, chamomile's gentle, non-irritating nature makes it an excellent choice for soothing the sensitive conjunctiva and promoting healing. By reducing discomfort and inflammation, chamomile capsules provide effective relief from pink eye symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce swelling and redness associated with pink eye, making it more comfortable to manage.

Antibacterial property: Chamomile's antibacterial properties, particularly its flavonoids and terpenes, inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause pink eye, reducing the risk of infection and promoting healing.

Calming and soothing property: The apigenin and other flavonoids in chamomile have a calming effect on the eyes, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by pink eye, making it easier to rest and recover.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Chamomile flowers are a popular choice for making medicinal capsules for pink eye due to their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and itching.
Roots: The roots of the chamomile plant contain apigenin, an antioxidant that has been shown to have antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating bacterial conjunctivitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers and dry them according to the traditional method within two weeks of harvest.
Grind 500 grams of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix one part powdered matricaria chamomilla with three parts carrier material such as silica gel or cornstarch in a large bowl for uniform blending.
Fill empty #0 capsules with 250 milligrams of the herbal blend mixture and seal them immediately to preserve potency and freshness.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to six months from the date of filling to ensure optimal shelf life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oats capsules helps with pink eye because of their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The beta-glucans present in oats have been shown to reduce redness, swelling, and discharge associated with conjunctivitis. Additionally, the antioxidants in oats capsules help to calm the eyes and promote healing by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation. By taking these capsules orally, the body absorbs the beneficial compounds that are then transported to the affected area, providing natural relief from pink eye symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Oat extracts have been shown to reduce inflammation and swelling associated with pink eye, helping to alleviate discomfort and promote healing.
Antimicrobial properties: Herbal oats contain compounds that have antimicrobial effects, which can help combat bacterial infections and prevent the spread of pink eye.

Soothing and emollient properties: Oat extracts are known for their soothing and moisturizing effects on the skin and mucous membranes, making them effective at calming redness, itching, and discharge associated with pink eye.
remedy_parts: Roots: Oat roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Seeds: Oat seeds contain beta-glucans, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healing in ocular infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest avena sativa seeds and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix the powdered avena sativa seeds with distilled water in a ratio of 1:20 for 10 minutes.
Add honey as a natural preservative and anti-inflammatory agent to the mixture in a dosage of 5 grams for every 100 grams of mixture for 5 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the avena sativa-honey mixture using a capsule filler machine for 3 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to pink eye that are treated with herbal capsules are conjunctivitis, blepharitis, and meibomian gland dysfunction. Herbal capsules containing ingredients such as Echinacea, Goldenseal, and Eyebright have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort. They may also soothe dryness and itching associated with these conditions. Additionally, herbal supplements rich in omega-3 fatty acids can support eye health and promote healing.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with pink eye is a blend of Eyebright, Goldenseal, and Echinacea. Eyebright is known for its anti-inflammatory properties, while Goldenseal's berberine has antimicrobial effects that combat bacterial infections. Echinacea, on the other hand, supports the immune system and helps the body fight off infections. Taking these capsules together can help reduce redness, swelling, and discharge associated with pink eye, promoting faster healing and recovery. Consult a healthcare professional before using any herbal supplements.

remedies.remedy_desc: Euphrasia officinalis capsules helps with pink eye because they contain the herb Eyebright, which has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated eyes. The capsules help reduce redness, swelling, and discharge associated with pink eye. Eyebright's antiseptic properties also combat bacterial infections that can cause pink eye, promoting a faster recovery and reducing the risk of complications. This natural remedy provides a gentle, non-invasive solution for alleviating pink eye symptoms and promoting overall eye health.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce inflammation and combat the infection causing pink eye.
Caffeic acid: This phenolic compound has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to soothe the eyes and reduce redness associated with pink eye.
Ursolic acid: This triterpene has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce inflammation and fight the infection causing pink eye.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and combat the infection causing pink eye.
Flowers: The flower extracts have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in treating pink eye symptoms.
Stems: The stem extracts contain compounds that help to soothe and calm the affected area, reducing redness and discomfort associated with pink eye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of fresh euphrasia officinalis leaves and flowers to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the harvested material in a low-temperature oven at 50 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Grind 10 grams of dried euphrasia officinalis into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered euphrasia officinalis with 10 grams of vegetable cellulose to create a uniform blend.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the herbal blend and seal them securely.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea capsules helps with pink eye because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herb contains compounds like alkylamides and polysaccharides that stimulate the immune system, reducing inflammation and promoting a faster recovery. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea has antimicrobial properties that help combat the underlying bacterial or viral infection causing the pink eye. By supporting the body's natural defenses, Echinacea purpurea capsules can aid in alleviating symptoms and promoting a quicker resolution of the condition. This makes it a popular natural remedy for pink eye.
remedy_constituents: Iridoids: Iridoids in Echinacea purpurea help with pink eye by reducing inflammation and modulating the immune response to combat bacterial and viral infections.
Phenylethanoids: Phenylethanoids in Echinacea purpurea possess antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of pathogenic microorganisms, which can contribute to pink eye.
Cichoric acid: Cichoric acid in Echinacea purpurea has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce the severity of pink eye symptoms and promote wound healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary part used due to their high concentration of active compounds, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid derivatives, which have anti-inflammatory properties.
Buds: The buds are used for their rich content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds that may help reduce inflammation and combat infection.
Leaves: The leaves of Echinacea purpurea contain a variety of compounds, including phenolic acids and alkylamides, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of fresh echinacea purpurea roots and flowers when they are in full bloom.
Clean and dry the harvested materials by spreading them out in a single layer for 24 hours.
Grind 100g of the dried echinacea purpurea into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 1:1 ratio of the ground echinacea purpurea powder with 100g of 60-mesh cellulose powder.
Fill 60 empty gelatin capsules with 0.5g of the echinacea purpurea mixture each.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis capsules helps with pink eye because they contain the anti-inflammatory properties of the Calendula flower, which can soothe and calm the irritated eyes. The antimicrobial properties in Calendula help to combat bacterial and viral infections that cause pink eye. Additionally, its antioxidant properties help to reduce inflammation and promote healing, making it an effective natural remedy to alleviate symptoms of pink eye, such as redness, itching, and discharge, promoting a speedy recovery.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: They help with pink eye by reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the affected tissues.
Phenolic acids: They help by exhibiting antimicrobial properties that help combat the underlying infection causing pink eye.
Carotenoids (specifically, beta-carotene and lutein): They help by providing antioxidant protection to the eye and promoting the healing of damaged tissue.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the most commonly used part of Calendula officinalis for making capsules due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds that help soothe and treat pink eye.
Leaves: The leaves are sometimes used as a secondary ingredient, as they may contain some of the same medicinal properties as the flowers, although in smaller quantities.
(Optional) Roots: Some herbal remedies may use the roots, but they are not typically the first choice for treating pink eye, as they contain different types of bioactive compounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of fresh calendula officinalis flowers at peak bloom on a dry sunny day.
Dry the flowers in a single layer on paper bags or screens for 2 weeks.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice grinder.
Mix 2 teaspoons of the powder with 1 teaspoon of gelatin or vegetable cellulose in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture using a capsule filling machine or spoon.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia capsules helps with pink eye because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. It contains compounds like alkylamides and polyphenols that help soothe and calm the infected area. By reducing redness and swelling, Echinacea angustifolia capsules can provide relief from the discomfort associated with pink eye. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties can help combat bacterial and viral infections that cause the condition. This natural remedy can support the body's immune system and promote faster recovery.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the swelling and redness associated with pink eye.
Polyphenols: As antioxidants, polyphenols can help combat the oxidative stress and inflammation that contribute to the development and severity of pink eye.

Alkaloids (specifically, Cichoric Acid): Cichoric acid has been shown to have immunomodulatory effects, which can help stimulate the body's natural defenses and reduce the severity of pink eye symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary part used due to their high concentration of compounds that help to combat infection and inflammation associated with pink eye.
Barks: The barks of the Echinacea angustifolia plant contain anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective against pink eye's symptoms.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes, or underground stems, of the plant contain compounds that aid in reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, making them useful for pink eye treatment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried echinacea angustifolia roots and leaves from a certified organic source.
Dry the echinacea angustifolia roots and leaves in a temperature-controlled environment at 40 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Grind the dried echinacea angustifolia roots and leaves into a fine powder using a mill at 100 rpm.
Mix the powdered echinacea angustifolia with 100 grams of vegetable cellulose and 10 grams of silicon dioxide to create a uniform blend.
Fill 100 empty capsules with 500 milligrams of the echinacea angustifolia blend each using a capsule filler machine.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale capsules helps with pink eye because they contain comfrey, a natural anti-inflammatory agent that reduces swelling and soothes irritated conjunctiva. The capsules also contain mucilages, which form a protective barrier on the eye's surface, helping to prevent further irritation and promoting healing. Additionally, comfrey's antimicrobial properties help combat the underlying bacterial infection that often causes pink eye, promoting a speedy recovery and relief from discomfort. This natural remedy offers a safe and effective alternative to conventional treatments.
remedy_constituents: Allantoin: Helps reduce inflammation and promote healing of the conjunctiva, the membrane covering the white part of the eye and the inner eyelid.
Mucilages: Soothes and protects the mucous membranes in the eyes, reducing redness and discomfort associated with pink eye.
Saponins: Possesses antimicrobial properties, helping to combat bacterial and viral infections that cause pink eye.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their anti-inflammatory properties which help to reduce swelling and discomfort associated with pink eye.
Roots: Utilized for their soothing and protective properties to ease eye irritation.
Buds: Employed for their potential to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the affected area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried symphytum officinale root and stems from a reputable supplier.
Crush the harvested plant material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 gram of the plant powder with 9 grams of cellulose powder and 0.5 grams of magnesium stearate.
Fill 100 gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the mixture and seal them tightly.
Label the capsules with the date of preparation, ingredient name, and recommended dosage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hydrastis canadensis capsules helps with pink eye because of their antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb, also known as goldenseal, contains berberine, which has been shown to combat bacterial and fungal infections that cause pink eye. Additionally, goldenseal's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce redness and swelling associated with the condition. By addressing the underlying causes of pink eye, Hydrastis canadensis capsules can promote healing and alleviate symptoms, providing relief for those suffering from this uncomfortable and contagious condition.
remedy_constituents: Berberine: Exhibits antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce the bacterial load and alleviate symptoms associated with pink eye.
Hydrastine: Displays anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort associated with pink eye.
Vasaka: Exhibits antiseptic and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce the risk of infection and promote healing of the affected area.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary part used due to their high content of berberine, a compound with antimicrobial properties that can help combat pink eye infections.
Leaves: The leaves are used due to their secondary content of berberine and other antimicrobial compounds that aid in reducing inflammation and fighting off pink eye-causing pathogens.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes contain similar compounds as the roots and leaves, making them a viable alternative for producing capsules that treat pink eye.
remedy_recipe: Source high-quality hydrastis canadensis root, ensuring it is free from contaminants and heavy metals, with a minimum of 10% glycosides.
Dry the root material in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours to prevent degradation and preserve bioactive compounds.
Grind the dried root into a fine powder using a mill or grinder to increase surface area and bioavailability.
Mix the ground root powder with a suitable capsule filler, such as rice flour or tapioca starch, in a 1:2 ratio.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the hydrastis canadensis powder mixture, using a capsule filler machine to achieve consistent dosages of 300 mg per capsule.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera capsules helps with pink eye because of their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The gel extract of aloe vera, when taken in capsule form, can help soothe and calm the eyes, reducing redness and swelling associated with pink eye. Additionally, aloe vera's potent antibacterial properties can help combat bacterial infections that cause pink eye, promoting healthy healing and recovery. Its ability to regulate the immune system also aids in fighting off the underlying infection.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These compounds help reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, which can aid in the recovery from pink eye.
Polysaccharides: Polysaccharides, such as acemannan, exhibit anti-inflammatory properties and can help soothe the eyes, reducing discomfort associated with pink eye.
Phenolic acids: These acids, including cinnamic acid and caffeic acid, have antimicrobial properties that can help prevent the spread of infection and promote healing in the affected area.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The gel from the leaves of Aloe vera is used to make capsules for pink eye due to its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Leaves (Gel): The clear gel inside the leaves is also used to make capsules for pink eye because of its ability to reduce swelling and ease discomfort associated with pink eye.
Leaves (Pulp): The pulp from the leaves of Aloe vera is used to make capsules for pink eye due to its antibacterial properties that help combat pink eye infections.
remedy_recipe: Measure 500 grams of dried aloe vera powder and 250 grams of gelatin powder in separate containers for later use.
Mix 500 grams of dried aloe vera powder with 500 milliliters of distilled water in a large bowl to create a smooth paste.
Add 250 grams of gelatin powder to the bowl and stir until it is fully dissolved and no lumps remain.
Heat the mixture for 10 minutes to 15 minutes in a double boiler to facilitate gelatin melting.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the prepared aloe vera gel mixture and store them in a cool dry place for later use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis capsules helps with pink eye because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The gel from the Aloe vera plant contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial and viral infections. Additionally, Aloe vera's antimicrobial properties help to combat the underlying cause of pink eye, such as conjunctivitis, while its soothing properties calm the affected area, reducing redness, itching, and swelling, promoting faster healing and recovery.
remedy_constituents: Apolipoprotein A-IV: Helps with pink eye by reducing inflammation and improving the integrity of the eye's epithelial layer.
Bradykininase: Helps with pink eye by inhibiting the production of bradykinin, a compound that can lead to increased mucus production and swelling in the eyes.
Anthraquinones (specifically, aloe-emodin): Helps with pink eye by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in reducing redness, swelling, and the risk of infection.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds like aloe-emodin, which helps reduce redness and swelling associated with pink eye.
Gel from Leaves: Contains soothing and anti-inflammatory properties that help calm the affected area and reduce discomfort.
Juice from Leaves: Known for its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in treating and preventing the spread of pink eye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100-150 aloe barbadensis leaves from mature plants that are free of pests and diseases.
Cut the leaves into small pieces and remove the outer skin to expose the inner gel.
Blend the gel into a pulp using a food processor for 2-3 minutes.
Mix 100 grams of the pulp with 200 grams of gelatin and 20 grams of glycerin in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 0.5-1 gram of the herbal aloe barbadensis mixture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ulmus rubra capsules helps with pink eye because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to soothe and calm the irritated eyes. The herbal formula is rich in antioxidants and polyphenols that combat the underlying bacterial or viral infection causing the condition. By reducing inflammation and fighting off the infection, Ulmus rubra capsules promote healing and prevent further complications. This natural remedy also helps to alleviate symptoms such as redness, itching, and discharge associated with pink eye.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: They have antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce the bacterial load and alleviate symptoms associated with pink eye.
Tannins: They possess anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes, and prevent further infection.
Flavonoids: They have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and inflammation, and promote healing and recovery from pink eye.
remedy_parts: Barks: The barks of Ulmus rubra are used due to their antimicrobial properties, which help combat bacterial infections causing pink eye.
Leaves: The leaves of Ulmus rubra are used for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which aid in soothing and protecting the affected area.
Roots: The roots of Ulmus rubra are used for their ability to reduce swelling and promote healing, making them effective in treating pink eye symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of dried ulmus rubra bark by cutting branches during the autumn season when the bark is most dry.
Crush the dried ulmus rubra bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the powder with 10 grams of vegetable cellulose as a capsule filler and 5 grams of stearic acid as a binder.
Use a capsule machine to fill 100 capsules each containing 150 milligrams of the powder mixture and 50 milligrams of the binder.
Store the capsules in a cool dark place for 2 weeks before consuming 1-2 capsules twice a day.

remedies.remedy_desc: Trifolium pratense capsules helps with pink eye because they contain the herb Red Clover, which is rich in isoflavones. These isoflavones have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes. Additionally, Red Clover has antimicrobial properties that can combat bacterial or viral infections that often cause pink eye. By reducing inflammation and fighting off infection, Trifolium pratense capsules may help alleviate symptoms of pink eye, promoting a faster recovery and soothing red, itchy eyes.
remedy_constituents: Isorhapontigenin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce the inflammation and oxidative stress associated with pink eye.
Formononetin: As a prenylated isoflavone, formononetin may exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, contributing to the relief of pink eye symptoms.
Quercetin: This flavonoid has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate the symptoms of pink eye by reducing inflammation and protecting against oxidative damage.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in flavonoids and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate pink eye symptoms.
Flowers: Contain isoflavones, particularly formononetin and genistein, which have been used to treat pink eye due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Seeds: Used to produce extracts high in isoflavones and flavonoids, which have been traditionally used to treat pink eye and other eye conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh trifolium pratense flowers at the peak of blooming for optimal potency in 3-4 weeks after germination.
Dry the harvested flowers in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2-3 hours to remove moisture content.
Grind the dried trifolium pratense flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or grinder.
Mix 1 part of the trifolium pratense powder with 2 parts of a natural binding agent such as gelatin or starch.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250-500 mg of the trifolium pratense mixture and seal them tightly.
